rationale: The citing paper references Forehand and Deshpande (2001) only as a brief example in the introduction that shared social identification can affect consumer responses. It appears in a grouped, "see, e.g." style citation and is not subsequently used to build specific hypotheses, define key moderators, or guide measurement/analysis choices. The paper's core theoretical development relies more directly on other identity citations (for example, Forehand et al. 2002 and Reed 2004), so this specific citation functions as general background.
